title: Bonaga hircina
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant 'Bonaga hircina'. It's possible that this plant may be misspelled or not widely known.

However, I found information on 'Bonaga (Piper bonaga)' which is a plant in the pepper family (Piperaceae) and native to tropical forests in India and Southeast Asia. 

If that's the plant you were referring to, here's a paragraph:

Bonaga (Piper bonaga) is a perennial climber with medicinal properties. The health benefits of Bonaga include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can help in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Bonaga include piperine, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Bonaga include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
